creator: sdalu@sdalu.com
text:
I've got this problem on several computer but of course they have the same kind of configuration.

That's not a fresh FreeBSD 11 install, but one that has been upgraded multiple times, certainly started from around FreeBSD 7.

Yes that's intel architecture not ARM:
FreeBSD rork.home.sdalu.com 11.0-RELEASE-p7 FreeBSD 11.0-RELEASE-p7 #0 r312523: Fri Jan 20 15:22:07 CET 2017     root@rork.home.sdalu.com:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/RORK  amd64


rm -rf /usr/local /var/db/pkg/*
cd /usr/ports/lang/gcc5
env MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes make


root@rork:~ # setenv | grep ^L
LOGNAME=root

root@rork:~ # pkg info
binutils-2.28,1                GNU binary tools
bison-3.0.4,1                  Parser generator from FSF, (mostly) compatible with Yacc
gcc-ecj-4.5                    Eclipse Java Compiler used to build GCC Java
gettext-runtime-0.19.8.1_1     GNU gettext runtime libraries and programs
gettext-tools-0.19.8.1         GNU gettext development and translation tools
gmake-4.2.1_1                  GNU version of 'make' utility
gmp-6.1.2                      Free library for arbitrary precision arithmetic
help2man-1.47.4                Automatically generating simple manual pages from program output
indexinfo-0.2.6                Utility to regenerate the GNU info page index
isl-0.18                       Integer Set Library
libffi-3.2.1                   Foreign Function Interface
m4-1.4.18,1                    GNU M4
mpc-1.0.3                      Library of complex numbers with arbitrarily high precision
mpfr-3.1.5_1                   Library for multiple-precision floating-point computations
p5-Locale-gettext-1.07         Message handling functions
perl5-5.24.1_1                 Practical Extraction and Report Language
pkg-1.10.1                     Package manager
pkgconf-1.3.5,1                Utility to help to configure compiler and linker flags
python27-2.7.13_3              Interpreted object-oriented programming language
readline-6.3.8_1               Library for editing command lines as they are typed
texinfo-6.1.20160425_1,1       Typeset documentation system with multiple format output
zip-3.0_1                      Create/update ZIP files compatible with PKZIP



binutils:
	NLS            : on
	RELRO          : on
	STATIC         : off

gmp:
	CPU_OPTS       : on

perl
	DEBUG          : off
	DTRACE         : on
	GDBM           : off
	MULTIPLICITY   : on
	PERL_64BITINT  : on
	PERL_MALLOC    : off
	PTHREAD        : on
	SITECUSTOMIZE  : off
	THREADS        : on

python:
	DEBUG          : off
	IPV6           : on
	LIBFFI         : on
	NLS            : on
	PYMALLOC       : on
	THREADS        : on
	UCS2           : off
	UCS4           : on

/etc/make.conf
WRKDIRPREFIX=/usr/obj

A4=yes

JAVA_VERSION=1.8
APACHE_PORT= www/apache24

DEFAULT_VERSIONS+= perl5=5.24
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=  ruby=2.4
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+= mysql=101m
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+= pgsql=9.6
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=   php=71
#DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=   gcc=5
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=   bdb=6

from /usr/obj/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/libcpp/config.h:
/* Define to `int' if <sys/types.h> does not define. */
#define ptrdiff_t int
